rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHE BAUBERT ET AL VS. VERONICA STORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHE BAUBERT, ALEYDA BAUBERT MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Assuming without deciding that a party against whom no claim is alleged or relief sought in a pleading is permitted to seek the striking of a portion of the pleading, the Bauberts have failed to show sufficient grounds to strike any of the portions of Ms. Stork's cross-complaint that they seek to be stricken. Evidence Code 1152 and 1154 do not support the Bauberts' motion for two reasons. First, those sections pertain to admissibility of evidence and, unlike the sealing rules, do not establish a basis to strike any pleading language. Second, those sections only make settlement information inadmissible for specified subjects, not for all purposes, and the references to the June 22, 2020 demand letter are not made for any of those prohibited purposes. The references to the demand letter are, at a minimum, relevant to Ms. Stork's indemnity claim against Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHE BAUBERT ET AL VS. VERONICA STORK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT SIDE, INC., LOUKIA KARNERIS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all three causes of action. While they could have been more clearly alleged, all causes of action for elder abuse, breach of contract and UCL violation are adequately alleged. The elder abuse claim sufficiently alleges financial elder abuse based on the taking of money from Ms. Stork with the intent to defraud and Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris knew or should have known that their failure to disclose the encroachment problem to the Bauberts would likely be harmful to Ms. Stork. The breach of contract claim sufficiently alleges a breach of the portion of the parties' contract requiring Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris to "exercise reasonable effort and due diligence to achieve the purposes of this Agreement." The UCL claim sufficiently alleges a viable claim based on the fraud prong of that statute and seeks the available remedy of restitution of some or all of the commission Ms. Stork paid to Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHE BAUBERT ET AL VS. VERONICA STORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT SIDE, INC., LOUKIA KARNERIS MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED as to all portions of the first amended complaint that are sought to be stricken. The portion of this motion that are based on the assertion that Ms. Stork's elder abuse claim is invalid lacks merit given the overruling of Side, Inc. and Ms. Karneris' demurrer to that claim. Ms. Stork's request for contract attorney's fees is properly grounded on the fees provision of the parties' contract and Civil Code 1717. Hs. Stork's request for attorney's fess per CCP 1021.6 is properly grounded on her indemnity claim. Ms. Stork's request for pre-judgment interest is properly ground on both Civil Code 3287 (a) and (b). , damages and interests are supported by factual allegations in the operative cross-complaint. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: PASQUALE DIANDA VS. LAN LE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2021 line .5 PLAINTIFF PASQUALE DIANDA Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Hearing required. The hearing will begin at 2pm, not 9:30am, and will consist of two parts: a non-argument site inspection and an in-court hearing. The parties are required to meet Judge Kahn at 2pm in front of 792 Hayes Street. Each side will have up to ten minutes to point out, without argument, relevant current physical features of the two properties and the locations of relevant previously existing and proposed structures. The in-court hearing will take place approximately half an hour after the conclusion of the site inspection. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
